_note: DUMMY sample registry for the template. Replace channel tokens, cronJobId, and jobs with your own. Channel IDs are placeholders (e.g. <CHANNEL_GENERAL>) — real deployments use the chat platform's channel IDs.

policy.sourceOfTruth: This file is the deterministic registry for ALL cron jobs (category: reminder | report | automation). Cron job names/prompts must not be inferred; this metadata is the source of truth. New cron jobs MUST be registered here at creation time.
policy.summaryRule: Weekly reminder summaries include only jobs with category=reminder, enabled=true, and weeklySummary.targets containing the requested target.
policy.privacyRule: A reminder is never summarized into a channel unless that channel is explicitly listed in weeklySummary.targets.
policy.driftRule: scripts/cron_registry_check.py compares this registry against `openclaw cron list --json` weekly — every job, LLM or command, is queried the same way. Registry edits do NOT sync to cron; fix drift manually in whichever side is wrong.
policy.reviewRule: Each job has reviewBy (YYYY-MM-DD). When overdue, decide keep/change/remove, then bump reviewBy.
policy.executionRule: Each job declares agentTurn. agentTurn=false means the job is a pure relay (a deterministic script's stdout posted verbatim, no LLM judgment) — create it with `openclaw cron add --command <shell>` so it runs on the Gateway and never consumes an agent turn. agentTurn=true jobs need LLM judgment/generation — create them with `--message` so they run through the LLM cron. Decide agentTurn first when adding a new periodic job: no LLM judgment needed → --command. Both kinds are the same `openclaw-cron` mechanism and show up identically in `openclaw cron list --json`, which is what keeps this portable across hosts (dev Mac or a VPS).

jobs.title: Weekly memory tidy-up
jobs.purpose: Run skills/memory-maintenance to keep the L0 index thin, fix dead pointers, and distill old daily logs.

jobs.title: Morning standup nudge
jobs.purpose: Remind the owner of today's top priority and any calendar events in the next 24h.

jobs.title: Morning calendar relay
jobs.purpose: Pure relay: `openclaw cron add --command '<script that prints today's calendar>' --announce` posts stdout verbatim. No LLM judgment needed, so no agent turn is spent.
